name: Irish Cream and Chocolate Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
This can be made a day ahead and freezes well.
CRUST: In a bowl, combine the crushed chocolate wafers and melted butter. Press into a 13 x 9 inch glass baking dish. Refrigerate.
FILLING: In a saucepan, cook the gelatin, salt, coffee, and milk, stirring constantly over medium heat until hot and the coffee is dissolved. Reduce heat. Stir some of the coffee mixture into the egg yolks, then pour the egg mixture back into the saucepan. Cook, stirring frequently, until the mixture thickens slightly (4-5 minutes). Remove from heat and add the liqueur. Using an electric mixer or food processor, blend the cream cheese and 1/4 cup of sugar until smooth. Slowly blend in the coffee mixture. Chill, stirring occasionally, until it reaches the consistency of raw egg whites, about 1-1.5 hours.
Meanwhile, melt the chocolate in a double boiler over hot water. Let cool. Beat egg whites until soft peaks form. Gradually add the remaining sugar and beat until stiff peaks form. Gently fold the egg whites into the coffee mixture. Using a large spoon, alternately spoon the two mixtures onto the chocolate crust. Use a knife to swirl the chocolate through.
